reasoning: Based on comprehensive analysis of available data through October 2025, the probability of >10x spike in Venezuela violence against civilians is very low. Key evidence: (1) ACLED Conflict Index showed Venezuela improved ranking in July 2024, (2) criminal groups have migrated out reducing overall violence, (3) reference threshold of 105+ events would require unprecedented escalation from baseline of ~9.5 events/30 days, (4) political repression continues but tends to be targeted rather than mass-scale ACLED-classified violence. Uncertainty increases with forecast horizon due to unknown triggers (elections, economic shocks, external intervention). Initial probability set at 0.12 for Nov 2, rising slightly to 0.14 (Nov 25) and 0.18 (Jan 24) as longer-term uncertainties accumulate.
